bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid by holding firm on the best value for every issue. The opponent’s opening offer gives me very low utility and provides no evidence of concessions yet, so I should counter with a strong anchor using only valid domain values.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid rather than making large concessions. The opponent has only moved slightly from all valueF to issueA:valueE while keeping the rest at valueF, which gives me very low utility and does not yet justify major concessions; I will make a small non-repeating concession on issueC while holding my strongest positions.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, and the opponent’s offers remain extremely low utility for me with no meaningful concession toward my preferred values. I am therefore targeting a high-utility bid while avoiding an exact repeat of my previous offer, making only a small concession on issueC rather than giving up value on my highest-weight issueA.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06, while making a small concession by varying issueC rather than giving up my most important issueA or other top values. The opponent has only slightly improved on issueA and is still holding low-value positions for me on issueB, issueC, and issueD, so their offers do not yet justify a major concession.

I am still targeting a high utility bid above 0.8 because it is very early in the negotiation, but I am making a small concession by moving issueB off my top value while holding firm on the most important issueA and valuable issueD. The opponent has improved somewhat for me over time, especially on issueA and issueC, but their latest offer is still far below my acceptable range, so only limited concession is warranted.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around the early-game range, since t=0.10 leaves plenty of time and the opponent is only gradually conceding. Their offers have improved for me mainly on issueA and issueC, while they keep pushing issueB=valueF, so I will hold firm on my most important issueA and on issueB/D while making a moderate concession on issueC.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility bid while making a meaningful concession on the issue where the opponent has been most consistent: issueB=valueF. Their offers show real concession on issueA and some movement on issueC, but they have held issueB at valueF throughout, so I will concede there while holding my high-value positions on issueA, issueC, and issueD.

I am targeting a still-high utility because it is early (t=0.14), but I am making a meaningful concession on issueB where the opponent has consistently held valueF. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA up to my top valueA and partly on issueC, so I will hold issueA and issueD while offering issueB=valueF and a moderate issueC concession.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.70 because it is early in the negotiation, while acknowledging the opponent has conceded substantially on issueA but remains firm around issueB=valueF and issueC=valueC/F. I am holding issueA and issueD at my strongest values, conceding issueB to their repeated valueF, and making only a small variation on issueC to avoid simply repeating my last bid.

At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.69 rather than accepting the opponent’s latest lower-value offer. The opponent has clearly conceded on issueA up to my top value, while holding issueB at valueF and often proposing issueC=valueC, so I concede on issueB and issueC but hold firm on issueD where their movement is still uncertain.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70 because it is only t=0.20, so I should not concede too deeply yet. The opponent has clearly pushed issueB toward valueF and issueC toward valueC while conceding substantially on issueA, so I keep my high-priority issueA at valueA, concede issueB to valueF, and keep issueD at my best valueA for now while slightly varying issueC to avoid repeating my exact last bid.

At t=0.22 it is still early, so I am targeting a high but not maximal utility around 0.69 while avoiding simply repeating my previous bid. The opponent has steadily conceded on issueA up to my best value and has shown willingness around issueC=valueC/valueD, but they remain firm on issueB=valueF and push for low issueD values; I will concede issueB and meet them on issueC while holding issueD at my top value.

I am targeting a utility around 0.70, since it is still early enough to hold firm on my highest-weight issueA and valuable issueD while conceding issueB to valueF. The opponent has repeatedly held issueB at valueF and has sometimes offered issueA: valueA and issueC: valueD/C, so this bid uses those signals while not yet giving up issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.694 by keeping my most important issueA at valueA and holding issueD at valueA, while conceding issueB to valueF and matching the opponent’s recent issueC valueC. At t=0.26 it is still early enough not to concede on issueD, but the opponent’s repeated offers suggest they strongly prefer issueB: valueF and are willing to give issueA: valueA.

I am targeting a utility around 0.70, conceding on issueB and partly on issueC while holding firm on the higher-value issueA and issueD. At t=0.28 there is still time, so I should not drop to the opponent’s low-utility pattern yet; their offers show repeated insistence on issueB=valueF and movement around issueC/valueC-D, with no natural-language messages to add reliable information.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.69 for me, since we are still only at t=0.30 and there is no need to make a major concession yet. The opponent has consistently pushed issueB to valueF and has often offered issueC as valueC/valueD, so I concede issueB and align issueC to valueC while holding firm on high-value issueA and issueD.

At t=0.32 I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.59, conceding on lower-priority issueB and issueD while holding firm on high-priority issueA. The opponent has repeatedly signaled willingness to accept issueB: valueF and issueC: valueC, and their latest offer moved issueD to valueC, so I match those while asking for issueA: valueA, which they have offered before.

I am targeting a utility just under 0.60, since we are still in the early-mid phase and there is no need to concede all the way to the opponent’s latest offer yet. The opponent has been very firm on issueB=valueF, while gradually improving issueA and issueD for me, so I concede issueB but hold issueA=valueA and ask for a modest improvement on issueC/issueD versus their current pattern.

I am targeting about 0.59 utility, conceding mainly on issueB where the opponent has been completely firm on valueF while holding issueA at valueA because it is my highest-weight issue. At t=0.36 there is still time, so I am not dropping to the opponent’s latest 0.50-ish offer, but I am matching their repeated preferences on issueB and issueC and offering a moderate concession on issueD. The opponent’s offers show real movement on issueA and issueD, but no movement on issueB, so this bid focuses on a plausible tradeoff rather than accepting their current position.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.56 while preserving my most important issue, issueA=valueA, and conceding on issueB where the opponent has consistently held valueF. The opponent has signaled willingness around issueC=valueC and issueD=valueD/C, including a prior offer of this exact structure, so this is a pragmatic concession without dropping too far this early.
